title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.title: Pineapple Bun Recipe (Bo Lo Bao) - Authentic Chinese Pastry

description: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a cherished Hong Kong classic, renowned for its distinctive sweet, crumbly topping that resembles a pineapple's skin. Beneath this delightful golden crust lies a wonderfully soft and fluffy milk bread, offering a perfect balance of textures and flavors. Despite its name, this iconic pastry contains no pineapple fruit, making it a delicious surprise for those new to Chinese baking.

instructions.title: Prepare the Bread Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 250g flour, 30g sugar, 3g yeast, and 2g salt. Gradually add 130ml warm milk and 1 egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Turn onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Incorporate 25g softened butter and knead for another 5 minutes until fully absorbed and the dough passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Proofing
instructions.text: Place the kneaded dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it proof in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size. This step is crucial for a soft texture.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough proofs, prepare the topping. In a separate bowl, cream together 50g softened butter and 60g sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in 1 egg yolk and 1/2 tsp vanilla extract until well combined. Gradually add 100g flour and 10g milk powder, mixing until a soft, crumbly dough forms. Do not overmix. Cover and refrigerate until needed.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release air. Divide the dough into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Take the chilled topping, divide it into 8 equal portions, and flatten each portion into a disc large enough to cover the top of a dough ball. Place a topping disc over each dough ball, gently pressing and shaping to cover the top half.

instructions.title: Second Proofing
instructions.text: Arrange the shaped buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them. Cover loosely with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let them proof in a warm place for another 45-60 minutes, or until visibly puffy. Preheat your oven to 180C (350F) during the last 15 minutes of proofing.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Before baking, whisk together 1 egg yolk and 1 tsp milk for the egg wash. Carefully brush the egg wash over the topping of each bun, taking care not to let it drip down the sides onto the bread part. Bake for 15-18 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and cracked, and the bread is cooked through. Transfer to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

tips: For the crispiest topping, ensure your butter for the topping is soft but not melted, and refrigerate the topping dough briefly before shaping to make it easier to handle.
Do not over-knead the topping dough; mix just until combined to maintain its crumbly texture. Overmixing can make it tough.
To achieve the signature cracked pattern, score the topping lightly with a knife or fork before applying the egg wash, but be careful not to cut into the bread dough underneath.
article:
Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) is a beloved staple in Hong Kong bakeries and dim sum restaurants worldwide. Despite its misleading name, this iconic pastry contains no pineapple. Instead, it earns its moniker from the distinctive golden, cracked, and crumbly sugar crust that resembles the skin of a pineapple. Beneath this sweet, crunchy exterior lies a pillowy soft, slightly sweet bread, making for a truly irresistible combination of textures and flavors. It's a testament to the magic of Chinese baking, offering comfort and delight in every bite. If you've ever wanted to recreate that authentic bakery experience at home, you're in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to master the art of making your very own Pineapple Buns.

## What is Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao)?

Bo Lo Bao, or Pineapple Bun, is a classic Cantonese pastry that originated in Hong Kong. It consists of two main components: a soft, enriched milk bread base and a crispy, sugary, cookie-like topping. The topping, usually made from sugar, eggs, flour, and butter, is spread over the dough just before baking. As it bakes, the topping expands and cracks, forming that characteristic "pineapple" pattern and creating a delightful contrast with the tender bread underneath. While typically enjoyed plain, some variations include a slab of cold butter inserted into a freshly baked bun (known as "Bo Lo Yau"), or savory fillings like char siu (BBQ pork). Its universal appeal lies in its simple yet perfect harmony of textures – crunchy, soft, and slightly chewy – and its balanced sweet flavor. This humble bun has secured its place as a quintessential part of Hong Kong's culinary identity, enjoyed for breakfast, afternoon tea, or as a comforting snack any time of day.

## Ingredients for Homemade Pineapple Buns

Crafting the perfect Pineapple Bun at home requires fresh ingredients and a little patience. Here’s what you’ll need for a fantastic homemade Pineapple Bun Recipe:

For the Milk Bread Dough:
*   300g bread flour (high protein flour is best for chewiness)
*   40g granulated sugar
*   4g instant dry yeast
*   4g salt
*   1 large egg (around 50-55g), lightly beaten
*   120ml warm milk (around 35-40°C)
*   30g unsalted butter, softened

For the Crumb Topping:
*   60g unsalted butter, softened
*   80g granulated sugar
*   1 large egg yolk
*   1/2 tsp vanilla extract (optional, but adds a lovely aroma)
*   120g all-purpose flour
*   1/4 tsp baking powder
*   Pinch of salt

For the Egg Wash:
*   1 egg yolk
*   1 tbsp milk or water

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Bo Lo Bao

Making Bo Lo Bao is a rewarding process, and following this detailed Pineapple Bun Recipe will ensure delicious results.

1.  **Prepare the Milk Bread Dough**:
    *   In a large mixing bowl, combine bread flour, sugar, yeast, and salt. Mix well.
    *   Add the beaten egg and warm milk to the dry ingredients. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.
    *   Turn the dough out onto a clean surface and knead for about 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. If using a stand mixer, knead with a dough hook on medium-low speed for 8-10 minutes.
    *   Add the softened butter and continue kneading for another 5-7 minutes until the butter is fully incorporated and the dough is very smooth, elastic, and passes the windowpane test (you can stretch a small piece thin enough to see light through it without tearing).
    *   Form the dough into a ball, place it in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

2.  **Make the Crumb Topping**:
    *   While the dough is rising, prepare the topping. In a medium bowl, cream together the softened butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
    *   Beat in the egg yolk and vanilla extract until well combined.
    *   Sift in the all-purpose flour, baking powder, and salt. Mix until just combined and a soft, crumbly dough forms. Do not overmix.
    *   Gather the topping dough into a disc, wrap it in plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up. This makes it easier to handle.

3.  **Shape the Buns**:
    *   Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air.
    *   Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces (around 60-70g each). Roll each piece into a smooth ball.
    *   Place the dough balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them.

4.  **Apply the Topping**:
    *   Take the chilled crumb topping from the fridge. Divide it into the same number of pieces as your dough balls.
    *   Take one piece of topping and flatten it between two sheets of plastic wrap or parchment paper into a disc roughly the size of your dough ball. It should be thin but not breaking.
    *   Carefully peel off one side of the plastic wrap, invert the topping disc onto a dough ball, and gently peel off the remaining plastic wrap.
    *   Lightly press the topping onto the dough ball, ensuring it covers the top and sides, but not all the way to the bottom. Repeat for all buns.

5.  **Second Proofing**:
    *   Cover the topped buns loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let them rise again in a warm place for another 45-60 minutes, or until visibly puffy (about 1.5 times their original size).

6.  **Bake the Pineapple Buns**:
    *   Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F) about 15 minutes before the end of the second proofing.
    *   Prepare the egg wash by whisking the egg yolk with milk/water.
    *   Just before baking, gently brush the tops of the buns (on the topping) with the egg wash.
    *   Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and cracked, and the bread underneath is cooked through.
    *   Transfer the baked buns to a wire rack to cool slightly.

## Pro Tips for the Perfect Pineapple Bun

Achieving bakery-quality Pineapple Buns at home is within reach with a few expert tricks:

*   **Don't Rush Proofing**: Proper proofing is crucial for soft bread. Ensure your dough doubles in size during the first proof and becomes visibly puffy during the second. A warm, humid environment (like a slightly warmed, turned-off oven with a cup of hot water) can help.
*   **Chill the Topping**: A well-chilled topping is easier to handle and prevents it from melting too quickly into the dough before baking, ensuring a distinct crust.
*   **Handle Topping Gently**: The topping can be delicate. Flatten it carefully and transfer it to the dough using plastic wrap or parchment paper to prevent it from breaking.
*   **Egg Wash for Shine**: The egg wash isn't just for color; it helps create that beautiful, glossy finish on the topping. Apply it just before baking.
*   **Oven Temperature Matters**: A consistent oven temperature is key for the topping to crack properly and the bread to bake evenly. Use an oven thermometer if unsure.
*   **Do Not Overbake**: Overbaking will dry out your buns. Keep an eye on them and remove them once the topping is golden and the bread feels light.
*   **The Best Pineapple Bun Recipe Starts with Good Ingredients**: Use good quality bread flour for the dough and fresh butter for both the dough and topping for the best flavor and texture.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Pineapple Buns are incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. Traditionally, they are served warm, fresh from the oven, allowing the crispy topping to truly shine against the soft bread.

*   **Plain and Simple**: The most classic way to enjoy Bo Lo Bao is by itself, appreciating the interplay of textures and the subtle sweetness.
*   **With Butter (Bo Lo Yau)**: For an indulgent treat, slice a warm bun horizontally and insert a thick slab of cold, unsalted butter. The butter melts into the warm bread and topping, creating a heavenly richness.
*   **Breakfast or Brunch**: Pair your Pineapple Bun with a cup of Hong Kong-style milk tea, coffee, or even a simple glass of milk for a comforting start to your day.
*   **Afternoon Tea**: These buns are a perfect accompaniment to a leisurely afternoon tea, alongside other pastries or savory snacks.
*   **Filled Variations**: While not traditional for the plain version, you can adapt your Pineapple Bun Recipe to include fillings. For example, some bakeries offer them with a sweet custard filling, a savory char siu pork filling, or even a coconut paste.

## Variations and Substitutions for Pineapple Buns

While the classic Pineapple Bun is a masterpiece on its own, there are several ways to experiment with this popular Chinese baking delight:

*   **Fillings**: As mentioned, consider adding a filling. After dividing the dough into balls, flatten each piece, place a spoonful of custard, char siu, or red bean paste in the center, and then seal the dough around the filling before shaping it back into a ball. Proceed with the topping as usual.
*   **Flavoring the Topping**: For a subtle twist, you can add a pinch of citrus zest (lemon or orange) to the crumb topping mixture for a brighter flavor. A tiny amount of almond extract could also add depth.
*   **Whole Wheat Flour**: For a slightly healthier version, you can substitute a portion (up to 25-30%) of the bread flour with whole wheat flour. This will result in a denser bun, so adjust milk slightly if needed.
*   **Dairy-Free Options**: While challenging, you can attempt to make this Pineapple Bun Recipe dairy-free by using plant-based milk and vegan butter substitutes. The texture and flavor might differ slightly but can still yield a delicious result.
*   **Sweetness Adjustment**: Feel free to slightly reduce the sugar in both the dough and the topping if you prefer a less sweet bun. However, remember sugar plays a role in the texture and browning.

Making Pineapple Buns at home is a wonderfully rewarding experience that connects you with a cherished piece of Hong Kong's culinary heritage. With this detailed Pineapple Bun Recipe, you're well-equipped to bake these golden-crusted, soft-centered delights right in your own kitchen. Enjoy the process, and savor every bite of your homemade Bo Lo Bao!
